Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding spring:
1. If the leaf spring has a shape of uniformly varying width then the bending stress will also be varying.
2. Leaf springs are commonly used in car, railway wagons etc.
3. Carriage springs are used normally as shock absorbers.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A leaf spring designed with a uniformly varying width is known as a "beam of uniform strength". In
such a design, the bending stress is constant or uniform throughout all cross-sections of the spring.

Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
1. Euler's formula is valid only for long columns while Rankine's formula is valid only for short columns.
2. Euler's formula does not take into account the effect of direct compressive stress.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Rankine's formula: This empirical formula is applicable for columns of all lengths, including
short, intermediate and long columns. It accounts for both crushing failure (in short columns)
and buckling failure (in long columns).

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Priming is necessary in

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Priming of a centrifugal pump is the operation of filling the suction pipe, casing of the pump and
a portion of the delivery pipe completely from outside source with the liquid to be raised, before
starting the pump, to remove any air, gas or vapour from those parts of the pump.

Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Match List-I (Device) with List-II (Parameter used to measure) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Hot-wire anemometer: This device is used to measure fluid velocity and is particularly known
for its ability to measure the high-frequency fluctuations associated with turbulence.
• Orifice meter: An orifice meter is a type of differential pressure flow meter used to measure
the discharge (flow rate) of a fluid in a pipe.
• Pitot tube: The primary function of a Pitot tube is to measure the local velocity of a fluid at a
specific point in the flow.
• Preston tube: A Preston tube is a specialized instrument used to measure the difference in
pressure, which is then used to determine the boundary shear stress on a surface.

Q23 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

An air vessel is used in a reciprocating pump

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
An air vessel is a large closed chamber fitted to a reciprocating pump to eliminate pulsations of
pressure and discharge in suction and delivery pipes.
Main advantages of air vessels in reciprocating pump are:
• Air vessel on suction side:
(i) Reduces cavitation possibility
(ii) For a given minimum pressure head, the pump can run at higher speed
(iii) Suction pipe length can be increased
• Air vessel on delivery side:
(i) Almost constant delivery discharge is obtained
(ii) Reduction in friction loss and hence saving in power.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements related to matrix method of structural analysis:
1. Product of stiffness matrix and flexibility matrix with respect to the same coordinate system is a unit matrix.
2. Reciprocity in flexibility matrix and stiffness matrix can be derived using Maxwell's reciprocal theorem.
3. The diagonal elements of a stiffness matrix can be negative.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• For any stable structure, applying a force at a point will result in a displacement in the same direction. This physical principle ensures that all diagonal elements of the flexibility matrix are positive.
• The off-diagonal elements (Fij), where i ≠ j. represent the displacement at coordinate i caused by a unit load at coordinate j. These can be zero, negative or positive.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Displacement method of structural analysis is generally preferred over force method of structural analysis when:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The displacement method is best applied to structures with a high degree of static indeterminacy
and a low degree of kinematic indeterminacy. This is because the number of equilibrium equations
required to analyze the structure is equal to the kinematic indeterminacy, and it is more practical to
solve for a smaller number of unknowns (displacements here) than a number of unknowns (forces).

Q35 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A two hinged parabolic arch of span l is subjected to a uniformly distributed load of w per meter on whole span. The bending moment at  distance from left end is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
For a parabolic arch subjected to a uniformly distributed load (UDL) over its entire span, the
bending moment is zero at every point. This is because the arch's parabolic shape is a funicular
shape for a UDL, meaning the horizontal thrust from the arch's supports perfectly counteracts
the load's tendency to cause bending.

Q39 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Principle of virtual work can be used to determine the collapse load
2 Equilibrium condition, mechanism condition and yield condition are to be satisfied for the plastic methods of analysis.
3. Static method represents the lower limit to the true ultimate load and has a maximum factor of safety.
4. The static theorem satisfies the equilibrium and yield conditions.
Which of the above statements are correct in Plastic analysis of structure

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
For plastic analysis both, equilibrium and yield conditions are to be satisfied.
Static method uses maximum value of FOS and minimum ultimate load.
Static theorem satisfies the equilibrium and yield conditions.
Hence, all statements are correct.

Correct answer: C

as compared to lacing members, so the effective length of column is increased by 10%.

Q55 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Prestressing can control or even eliminate concrete tensile stress for specified loads
2. Concentric prestress is usually more efficient then eccentric prestress.
3. Variable eccentricity is usually preferable over constant eccentricity, from the view-points of both stress control and deflection control.
4. An advantage of equivalent load concept is that it leads the designers to select what is probably the best tendon profile for a particular loading.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Prestressing controls tensile stress: Prestressing applies a pre-compression to a concrete
member, which reduces or eliminates the tensile stresses caused by external loads. This is a
fundamental purpose of prestressed concrete.
• Variable eccentricity is preferred: Using a variable eccentricity allows the designer to create
a tendon profile that closely matches the bending moment diagram of the applied loads. This
leads to more efficient stress and deflection control throughout the beam's length.
• Equivalent load concept is beneficial: The equivalent load concept simplifies the analysis of
prestressed concrete and helps designers determine the ideal tendon profile to counteract
the effects of a gravity loading condition.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

The probability distribution to represent the completion of the project as per PERT analysis is

Correct answer: C
Solution

Q61 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding types of network:
1. Precedence networks are more widely used where repetitive tasks are involved.
2. Activity over arrows are considered better for the representation of complex projects.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A-O-N system (Precedence diagram): For the project involving higher repetitive nature of work
or series of operation, A-O-N network is to be preferred.
PERT (Program Evaluation and Review Technique) uses A-O-A diagram for research and development
type of projects. R & D works are complex as the time required for their completion is not fixed.
Hence, we can say that for complex projects A-O-A network diagrams are considered better.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Sulphate resisting cement can be used in marine construction.
2. High alumina cement is generally used in mass construction.
3. Low heat cement contains higher percentage of C3A and lower percentage of C2S.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Sulphate-resisting cement (SRC) is specifically designed for use in environments with high sulphate
concentrations, such as marine environments. It has a low content of tricalcium aluminate (C3A),
which is the compound most susceptible to sulphate attack.
High alumina cement evolves huge amount of heat and is thus not used in mass construction.
Low heat cement is manufactured by reducing the proportion of Tricalcium Aluminate (C3A) and
Tricalcium Silicate (C3S) and increasing the proportion of Dicalcium Silicate (C2S). The lower
percentage of C3A, directly leads to a lower heat of hydration.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q76 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In the process of brick manufacturing, the pug mill is used in which the following operation?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
A pug mill is a machine used to knead and mix clay, bringing it to a workable plasticity. This
process is called tempering. Weathering is the exposure of clay to the elements to soften it.
Blending involves mixing different clay types or additives. Burning is the final stage of brick
manufacturing where the bricks are hardened by heat.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

3. Vermiculite is an artificial light weight aggregate which produces high-strength and low shrinkage concrete.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Rough aggregate surface results in stronger bond, due to better interlocking
• Vermiculite is an artificial light weight aggregate which produces low strength and highshrinkage
concrete.

Correct answer: C

(c)
As fineness of cement increases, shrinkage increases.

3. Water absorption of bricks should not exceed 10%.
4. Chrome bricks are known as basic bricks.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
For refractory bricks, furnace is fired at temperature of more than 1700°C. Silica content should
be more than 40%. Water absorption of bricks should not exceed 10%.
Chrome bricks are known as neutral bricks.

Q82 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Match List-I (Quality parameters of water) with List-II (Titrants) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Alkalinity is measured by titrating the water sample with a strong acid, such as sulphuric acid (H2SO4), to a specific pH endpoint.
• Hardness is determined by the EDTA titration method, where EDTA
(Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid) is used to complex with the calcium and magnesium ions that cause hardness.
• Chloride content is typically measured using the Mohr’s method, which involves titrating the sample with silver nitrate (AgNO3).
• Dissolved oxygen is measured using the Winkler’s method, which involves a series of reactions followed by titration with sodium thiosulfate ( Na2S2O3)

Q85 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding dissolved oxygen in water bodies is/are correct?
1. The Streeter-Phelps DO sag equation is fundamentally a mass balance equation that models the oxygen deficit in a river over time.
2. The maximum amount of oxygen that can be dissolved in water (saturation value) is lower in cold water compared to warm water.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The Streeter-Phelps equation is a classic water quality model used to predict the concentration of dissolved oxygen downstream from a source of pollution. It is derived from a mass balance approach, considering two primary competing processes:
1. Deoxygenation 2. Reaeration
• The relationship between water temperature and dissolved oxygen saturation is inverse.
Cold water has a higher capacity to hold dissolved gases, including oxygen, than warm
water. For example, the saturation concentration of DO in fresh water at 0°C is about 14.6 mg/l,
while at 30°C it is about 7.6 mg/l. This is why thermal pollution (discharging hot water into
a river) can be harmful to aquatic life, as it reduces the available dissolved oxygen.

2. If fluoride content is more than 1.5 gm/litre then it results in de-colouration of teeth.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Fluoride content upto 1 ppm (1 mg/litre) helps to prevent dental cavities.
• If fluoride content is more than 1.5 ppm then it results in de-colouration of teeth.

Q88 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

Solution

Q89 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Detritus is the mixture of grit and organic matter.
2. Organic matter is subsequently removed from detritus by washing.
3. The cross sectional shape of grit channel will depend on the type of outlet flow control device used.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A detritus tank is a unit used in wastewater treatment that removes a mixture of grit and
some organic matter. While the biological definition of detritus is typically decomposing
organic matter, in the context of wastewater treatment, a detritus tank is designed to remove
a combination of both.
• The grit and other substances collected in grit chambers often contain a significant amount of
organic matter. To prepare the grit for disposal or reuse, it is sent to a grit washing process
that is specifically designed to separate and wash out the organic components.
• The design and cross-sectional shape of a grit channel are directly related to the flow control
device used at its outlet. For example, a rectangular grit chamber is used with a proportional
weir, while a parabolic cross-section is typically used with a Parshall flume to maintain a
constant flow velocity regardless of the flow rate.

4. Width of weaving section should be two traffic lane more than the average of entry and exit widths.

Each of the next Ten (13) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): When a thin flat plate is held parallel to a fluid stream then both pressure drag as well as viscous drag will be negligible.
Statement (II): The total drag will be entirely due to shear stress and pressure drag will be negligible.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q146 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (13) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Rounded aggregate is unsuitable for higher strength concrete.
Statement (II): Rounded aggregate results in high percentage of voids than angular aggregate.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Rounded aggregate results in minimum percentage of voids (32 to 45%) whereas angular aggregates
results in maximum percentage of voids (38 to 45%).

Q147 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (13) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Wide expansion joint are generally avoided while designing a rigid pavement.
Statement (II): During winter season, it is difficult to properly fill the expansion joints.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The purpose of expansion joint is to allow expansion of pavement due to temperature rise and in
winter season, when temperature is low, these gaps have to be filled so wide joints are avoided.

Each of the next Ten (13) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): The phenomenon of quick sand occurs mostly in coarse sand and gravels.
Statement (II): Quick sand condition does not occur in clay soils as their cohesion holds the grains together ever under upward flow at critical hydraulic gradient.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Phenomenon of quick sand can occur in fine sands or silts and can not occur in coarse soils and so
Statement (I) is wrong.

Solution
